name: cmake
title: cmake
description:
cross-platform, open-source make system
CMake is used to control the software compilation process using
simple platform and compiler independent configuration files. CMake
generates native makefiles and workspaces that can be used in the
compiler environment of your choice. CMake is quite sophisticated: it
is possible to support complex environments requiring system
configuration, pre-processor generation, code generation, and template
instantiation.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cmake because cross-platform, open-source make system.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cmake as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cmake [<options>] -B <path-to-build> [-S <path-to-source>]` as documented upstream for cmake.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cmake [<options>] <path-to-source | path-to-existing-build>` as documented upstream for cmake.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cmake --build <dir> [<options>] [-- <build-tool-options>]` as documented upstream for cmake.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cmake --install <dir> [<options>]` as documented upstream for cmake.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cmake with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cmake --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install cmake. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cmake [<options>] -B <path-to-build> [-S <path-to-source>]`. Further invocations from upstream: `cmake [<options>] <path-to-source | path-to-existing-build>`; `cmake --build <dir> [<options>] [-- <build-tool-options>]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: cross-platform, open-source make system.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: cross-platform, open-source make system.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install cmake
Install with: sudo apt install cmake
